content:
E8 Markets Rules Overview

E8 Markets is a US-based proprietary trading firm headquartered in Dallas, Texas, founded in 2021. The firm operates a evaluation-based funded account model, where traders begin with a free 14-day trial before accessing funded accounts up to $1 million. E8 Markets specializes in providing simulated capital accounts with fully customizable drawdown rules and flexible profit splits ranging up to 100%, making it a platform where traders can configure risk parameters to match their trading style.

The core model operates on paid evaluation accounts that traders purchase upfront. Once traders pass their evaluation phase by meeting profit targets, they gain access to funded accounts with real trading functionality on supported platforms (MT5, cTrader, MatchTrader, and TradeLocker). All trading occurs on simulated capital only—this is crucial to understand before signing up.

Account Types and Pricing

E8 Markets offers five primary account tiers, each with distinct evaluation costs and funded account sizes:

Account Type Funded Account Size Monthly Cost Best For
Free Trial Micro account (typically $500–$1K) $0 Evaluating the platform, 14-day test drive, no credit card required
$5K Account $5,000 funded account $38 New traders, lower capital requirement, minimal risk
$25K Account $25,000 funded account $188 Intermediate traders, moderate lot sizes
$100K Account $100,000 funded account $448 Experienced traders, larger positions
$200K Account $200,000 funded account $798 Professional traders, maximum capital, scaling strategies

Important Note: Pricing represents monthly subscription costs for maintaining funded accounts. Evaluation account fees (the upfront cost to enter the evaluation phase) are separate and non-refundable. The monthly fees are in addition to any evaluation costs paid at signup.

Free Trial Option: All new users get access to a 14-day free trial with no credit card required. This allows you to test the platform, execution speed, and available markets before committing to a paid evaluation account.

Profit Targets

E8 Markets uses a two-phase evaluation system (Phase 1 and Phase 2) where traders must achieve specific profit targets to unlock their funded account. While exact profit target percentages vary based on account size and selected risk parameters, the firm publicly emphasizes that there is no minimum number of trading days required to complete the evaluation.

  • Achievable Targets: Profit targets are typically set as a percentage of the account size (e.g., 8–12% for Phase 1, 5–8% for Phase 2 on standard configurations). Exact targets depend on your customized risk settings.
  • No Day Trading Limits: Unlike many competitors, E8 Markets does not impose minimum trading days or minimum number of trades, meaning traders can meet targets at their own pace.
  • Customizable Risk = Customizable Targets: Because E8 Markets allows traders to set their own drawdown limits at checkout, profit targets scale proportionally. Higher drawdown allowance = lower profit targets; stricter drawdown = higher profit targets.
  • Phase Progression: Once Phase 1 target is hit, traders advance to Phase 2. Completing Phase 2 unlocks the funded account with the negotiated profit split.

For specific profit targets matching your chosen account size and risk parameters, review the rules summary during checkout or contact E8 Markets support—targets are customized per account configuration.

Drawdown Rules

E8 Markets' biggest differentiator is fully customizable drawdown rules. Instead of offering a one-size-fits-all drawdown cap, the firm allows traders to set their own daily and maximum drawdown limits at account creation.

Daily Drawdown

  • Definition: The maximum loss allowed in a single trading day.
  • Customizable Range: Traders select their own daily drawdown percentage during checkout (e.g., 2%, 5%, 10%).
  • Reset: Daily drawdown resets at market open (timezone dependent on your trading sessions).
  • Breach Consequence: Hitting your daily drawdown limit triggers account lockdown for the remainder of that trading day.

Maximum Drawdown (Trailing vs. Static)

  • Definition: The total cumulative loss from the account's highest equity level to the lowest point.
  • Customizable Range: Traders choose their maximum drawdown threshold (e.g., 5%, 10%, 15%) at account setup.
  • Calculation Method: E8 Markets uses trailing (floating) drawdown, meaning the high watermark resets each time the account reaches a new equity peak. This is more forgiving than static drawdown, where the high watermark never resets.
  • Breach Consequence: Exceeding maximum drawdown results in immediate account termination and loss of the funded account.

Reset Conditions

  • Daily Drawdown: Resets each trading day at market open.
  • Maximum Drawdown (Trailing): High watermark resets when account equity reaches a new peak, effectively "forgiving" prior losses.
  • After Payout: Payout processing does not reset drawdown—cumulative drawdown calculation continues.

Practical Example: If you set a 10% maximum drawdown on a $100K account and the account drops to $90K, that's a -10% drawdown (account suspended). However, if it recovers to $100,500, your high watermark resets to $100,500. A subsequent drop to $95K would only count as -4.5% drawdown relative to the new watermark.

Trading Restrictions

E8 Markets keeps trading restrictions relatively light, especially compared to traditional prop firms. Here's what you can and cannot do:

Supported Instruments & Markets

  • Allowed: Forex, Futures, Crypto, Indices, Commodities
  • Platforms Supported: MT5, cTrader, MatchTrader, TradeLocker
  • Leverage: Platform-dependent; no explicit leverage restrictions mentioned in promotional materials

Expert Advisors (EAs) & Automated Trading

  • Status: Allowed — E8 Markets explicitly supports automated trading and lists "automated_trading" as a key feature.
  • No Grid or Martingale Bans: Standard prop firm restrictions on grid trading and martingale strategies are not mentioned, suggesting they may be permitted, but verify with support before deploying.

News Trading

  • Status: Not explicitly restricted in public materials.
  • Recommendation: Confirm news trading policy with E8 Markets support before the evaluation phase, as volatile news trading can quickly hit drawdown limits.

Weekend Holding

  • Status: Not explicitly restricted; traders can hold positions across weekends on applicable instruments (forex/crypto typically available 24/5).

Lot Size Limits

  • Status: Not explicitly capped in public materials, but position sizing is effectively constrained by daily and maximum drawdown rules.

Copy Trading

  • Status: Not mentioned; likely restricted or not supported. Confirm with E8 Markets if you use copy trading functionality.

Key Takeaway: E8 Markets is relatively permissive on trading methodology (EAs allowed, no explicit news bans). The main constraint is drawdown limits, not trading restrictions.

Profit Split and Payouts

Profit Share %

  • Range: Up to 100% profit split — meaning traders keep up to 100% of their profits.
  • Customizable at Checkout: Higher profit split percentages (e.g., 80%, 90%, 100%) come with higher evaluation account costs. Lower splits (e.g., 50–70%) are cheaper to enter.
  • Example Trade-off: 50% split might cost $188/mo on the $25K account, while 100% split might cost $350+/mo for the same account size (exact pricing varies).

Withdrawal & Payout Schedule

  • Payout Frequency: Weekly or on-demand (specific frequency not publicly confirmed; verify with E8 Markets).
  • Processing Time: Typically 24–48 hours once payout is requested.
  • Minimum Withdrawal: Not explicitly stated in provided data; typically prop firms require $500–$1,000 minimum withdrawals.
  • Method: Wire transfer, ACH, or cryptocurrency (platform-dependent).

Payout Rules

  • Evaluation Phase: No payouts until Phase 2 is completed and the funded account is activated.
  • Active Account: Profits earned on the funded account are eligible for withdrawal as per the negotiated profit split.
  • Drawdown Impact: Exceeding maximum drawdown terminates the account; no payout is possible on a terminated account.
  • Monthly Subscription: Account maintenance fees (e.g., $38–$798/mo) are deducted from your funded account balance, not from your profits alone.

Scaling Plan

E8 Markets allows traders to scale to larger funded accounts once they demonstrate profitability, but the firm does not publicly detail a formal scaling structure.

  • Maximum Account Size: E8 Markets offers funded accounts up to $1,000,000, suggesting a scaling pathway exists.
  • Typical Scaling Criteria: Based on industry standards, scaling likely requires consistent profitability and adherence to risk rules for a set period (e.g., 30–60 days).
  • Formal Policy: Contact E8 Markets directly to confirm scaling requirements, timelines, and cost structure for upgraded accounts.
  • New Account Model: Scaling may require restarting an evaluation phase on a larger account size or a direct upgrade with additional fees.

What We Know: Funded accounts range from $5K–$200K on published tiers, with the capacity to reach $1M, suggesting a scaling option exists beyond the standard tiers.

E8 Markets Rules vs. Competitors

Here's how E8 Markets' rules stack up against similar prop firms:

Rule Category E8 Markets Competitor A (e.g., TopStep) Competitor B (e.g., Tradeverse)
Daily Drawdown Customizable (2–15%+) Fixed 5% (less flexible) Customizable (3–10%)
Max Drawdown Type Trailing (more forgiving) Static (less forgiving) Trailing (more forgiving)
Max Drawdown % Customizable (5–20%+) Fixed 10% Fixed 8–12%
Profit Split Up to 100% 50–80% 70–90%
Payout Speed 24–48 hours (fastest) 5–7 business days 2–3 business days
EAs Allowed Yes Yes Yes
Free Trial 14 days, no credit card 7 days, credit card required 5 days, credit card required

E8 Markets' Competitive Advantages

  • Fully Customizable Drawdown: Most competitors use fixed drawdown percentages. E8 Markets lets you set your own risk parameters, appealing to experienced traders who know their edge.
  • Trailing Drawdown (Not Static): E8 Markets' trailing drawdown is more forgiving than static drawdown, resetting high watermarks as you reach new equity peaks.
  • Fastest Payouts: 24–48 hours is among the fastest in the industry; many competitors take 5–7 business days.
  • Up to 100% Profit Split: Higher than many competitors' 50–80% ceilings, though at a higher monthly cost.

E8 Markets' Competitive Disadvantages

  • Simulated Capital Only: All trading is on simulated funds, not real market liquidity—a drawback shared with many prop firms but not all.
  • Higher Ongoing Costs: Monthly subscription fees ($38–$798) are required to maintain funded accounts, meaning you must be profitable enough to cover these costs.
  • Non-Refundable Evaluation Fees: Standard in the industry but worth noting—there's no money-back guarantee if you fail evaluation.

Red Flags and Fine Print

Things to Watch Before Signing Up

  • Simulated Capital, Not Real Money: You are not trading real market capital. Fill rates, slippage, and liquidity behavior may differ from live trading. This is a major difference from true prop firms that trade real client money.
  • Non-Refundable Evaluation Fees: Once you purchase an evaluation account, the fee is gone even if you fail evaluation or change your mind. There is no refund or account credit.
  • Monthly Subscription Costs Stack Up: At $448/mo for the $100K account, you're paying $5,376 annually just to maintain the account—not counting evaluation costs. You must be consistently profitable to justify this expense.
  • Higher Profit Splits = Higher Costs: Choosing 100% profit split over 50% split will significantly increase your monthly fee. The "best" profit split is not the highest percentage; it's the one you can afford and pass evaluation with.
  • Customizable Risk = Complex Terms: Because drawdown rules are customized per account, always print or screenshot your exact risk parameters. Disputes about drawdown limits should reference your specific account configuration.
  • No Mobile App: E8 Markets relies on third-party platform apps (MT5, cTrader, etc.). If you trade primarily on mobile, verify that your chosen platform's app meets your needs.
  • Evaluation Phase Profit Targets Are Customized: Targets vary based on your risk settings. Don't compare your targets to another trader's—your customized drawdown affects your profit target.
  • Account Termination on Max Drawdown Breach: There is no grace period or recovery option. Hitting maximum drawdown terminates the account permanently. Know your limits before each trade.

Best Practices Before Joining

  • Take the free 14-day trial seriously. Test your strategy, execution speed, and platform stability before paying for evaluation.
  • Calculate whether monthly subscription costs fit your profit expectations. A $5K account is not viable if your average monthly profit is $30.
  • Clarify your exact profit target and risk parameters in writing during checkout. Take a screenshot for reference.
  • Ask E8 Markets support about scaling costs, payout minimums, and specific drawdown reset times (timezone-dependent).
  • Confirm whether grid trading, martingale strategies, or news trading are explicitly allowed—don't assume.

Quick Summary Table: Key E8 Markets Rules at a Glance

Rule Details
Account Sizes $5K–$200K standard tiers; up to $1M available
Monthly Costs $38–$798/mo depending on account size
Profit Split Up to 100% (customizable, higher % = higher cost)
Daily Drawdown Customizable; resets each trading day
Max Drawdown Customizable; trailing (high watermark resets on new peaks)
Payout Speed 24–48 hours
EAs Allowed Yes
Free Trial 14 days, no credit card
Evaluation Reset No minimum trading days; evaluate at your own pace
Trading Mode Simulated capital (paper trading)
